prompt:
Compare a topic document to its reconstruction from extracted facts.
Identify specific extraction problems against the Five Tests.

TOPIC DOCUMENT (source of truth):
━━━
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Hats will only be worn during playground time to protect from the sun.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Religious headwear may always be worn, provided it does not obscure the student's vision; it must be removed for all PE lessons for health and safety reasons.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Hair can be any length and any style. For PE, long hair must be tied back for safety.
━━━

RECONSTRUCTION (concatenated extracted facts):
━━━
## Wearing hats during playground time

Students are permitted to wear hats only during playground time to protect from the sun.

## Religious headwear permissions

Students may wear religious headwear at all times, provided that it does not obscure their vision. During PE lessons, religious headwear must be removed for health and safety reasons.

## Hair length and style

Students may have any length and style of hair, but during PE lessons, any long hair must be tied back for safety reasons.

━━━

Find up to 4 specific problems. For each, classify as:
- over-atomized: one logical unit split into too many facts
- under-atomized: multiple distinct topics merged into one fact
- missing-content: any obligation, permission, exception, or numerical value absent from reconstruction
- not-standalone: fails Test 1 — implied "who", "when", or "except what" without stating it explicitly
- not-canonical: fails Test 4 — navigation language, passive construction, redundant preamble
- not-template-ready: fails Test 5 — audience-specific or channel-specific framing baked in
- wrong-tags: topic tags missing or inconsistent with topic_slug
- other: any other quality issue

Quote exact text from the topic document when describing each problem.
If the reconstruction is good, return an empty problems list.
raw_response: {"problems":[{"problem_type":"not-canonical","description":"The phrase 'Students are permitted to wear hats only during playground time to protect from the sun.' uses a passive construction that is unnecessary. The original could instead use direct language like 'Hats will only be worn during playground time to protect from the sun.'"},{"problem_type":"not-standalone","description":"The statement 'Students may wear religious headwear at all times, provided that it does not obscure their vision.' should explicitly mention 'Religious headwear may always be worn' as it was in the original to avoid any confusion."}],"overall":"Most of the essential facts are present and well-organized, with some minor issues in extraction clarity and canonical structure."}

problems.description: The phrase 'Students are permitted to wear hats only during playground time to protect from the sun.' uses a passive construction that is unnecessary. The original could instead use direct language like 'Hats will only be worn during playground time to protect from the sun.'

The statement 'Students may wear religious headwear at all times, provided that it does not obscure their vision.' should explicitly mention 'Religious headwear may always be worn' as it was in the original to avoid any confusion.

parsed_output.overall: Most of the essential facts are present and well-organized, with some minor issues in extraction clarity and canonical structure.
